race.name: HovdenTour
race.tagline: Mountain skiing in Setesdal — a 42-kilometer tour through Southern Norway's finest high-altitude trails.

vitals.registration: Open registration; competition and fitness class options

climate.description: Hovden sits at approximately 800m in the Setesdal mountains of Southern Norway. The altitude provides reliable snow conditions from December through April. Race day temperatures typically range from -12°C to -2°C.
challenges: Mountain weather can be variable in Setesdal
Wind exposure on open mountain terrain

course.primary: A 42.5km course through mountain terrain at Hovden in the Setesdal region, with start and finish at Hovden. Participants choose competition or fitness class. Hovden offers 170km of prepared trails.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks on mountain terrain
features: Mountain terrain in the Setesdal region
Start and finish at Hovden village

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A scenic mountain marathon in Southern Norway's Setesdal region with reliable snow at altitude. Primarily a regional event with good infrastructure but limited international profile.

history.summary: HovdenTour has been a fixture of the Southern Norwegian ski calendar since approximately the 1990s. Hovden is one of Southern Norway's premier ski destinations, located in the Setesdal mountains of Agder county. The race leverages the resort's extensive 170km trail network to offer a mountain ski marathon accessible from Southern Norway's population centers.
notable_facts: Located in Hovden, one of Southern Norway's premier ski resorts
170km of prepared trails in the Hovden area
Setesdal mountain setting with reliable snow conditions
Multiple distances from 10km family race to 42.5km marathon

youtube_data.search_query: HovdenTour cross country ski race Hovden

videos.title: Hovden Tour 2019!!
videos.description: The whole season has been building up to this particularly weekend and day at Hovden. Hovde Tour 2019 was my big goal this season and it was a great experience. Looking forward to do more of this in the future.

videos.title: Road To Hovden Tour 2019 I Back to Basics
videos.description: I've decided to compete in Hovden Tour 2019 this season. A 42k cross country ski race. Been a while since I've done some x-country skiing so it was back to the basics the first time out!

videos.title: Road To Hovden Tour 2019 I Ski racing in Sweden
videos.description: I did an impulse deciton to enter Halvasan 2019 which is half the distance of the infamous Vasaloppet. The race was 45 km which is 3 km longer than Hovden Tour, but I think Hovden Tour has a tougher course. This was never the less a great test and I feel more and more ready for Hovden Tour.

videos.title: HOVDENTOUR #1 | VLOG 01
videos.description:
Filmed with iPhone XS and DJI mavic pro

//SONGS//

videos.curation_reason: Race vlog content filmed during Hovden Tour 2019 with high-quality filming equipment providing race atmosphere.

videos.title: Road To Hovden Tour 2019 I Vasaloppet Personal Best?
videos.description: Following Lars Theodorsen on his quest to improve his personal best at Vasaloppet this year. Will he manage to do it in the worst conditions Vasaloppet has seen in years?

videos.title: Road To Hovden Tour 2019 I Getting Wrecked In Oslo
videos.description: Met up with Lars and Ella in Oslo to do, what I thought, a nice and chill Sunday training session.  I was very wrong which I found out the hard way when I tried to keep up with them towards the infamous Skansebakken in the woods of Oslo.

videos.title: Shredding pow and shooting photos at Hovden
videos.description: It's not been best winter so far for shredding pow, but it's finally starting to look up. The forecast for Hovden said a lot of snow would come in, so I drove up to time the snowfall perfectly.

quotes.text: I was sure they said it's all downhill from there and then went a bit and suddenly I was up again I was like... royally pissed. It was just 20k going up no love no chill.
It's been my main goal this whole season, kind of big nervous but really really stoked just ready to do this. Hopefully finish, that's the main goal and hopefully don't lose either.
